headlineSummary: Hacker News sentiment on June 18, 2026 was mixed-neutral overall, with a close tie among Google Gemini, Anthropic, and OpenAI; Gemini drew both criticism and praise, Anthropic had the strongest volume of mixed reactions, and OpenAI leaned slightly positive [E1][E4][E6][E7][E8][E9][E10][E11].

ranking.summary: Google Gemini had mixed-neutral sentiment in a close tie, with limited evidence showing one strong praise note for the coding experience alongside complaints about pricing, a wrong answer, and a negative long-term outlook [E3][E4][E6][E7].

Anthropic also sat in the same close tie, with high-support mixed evidence: strong privacy rejection and some model-quality criticism were offset by praise for Claude features, connectors, and agentic coding capability [E1][E2][E5][E9][E10][E11].

OpenAI was mixed-neutral in the same close tie, with limited evidence pointing slightly positive because comments praised a business move and called the reporting impressive, though one privacy-focused comment rejected ChatGPT for sensitive data [E1][E7][E8].

evidence.excerpt: ChatGPT and Anthropic will never, ever get me to tie my Health Data to their systems, but I still believe in the capabilities of AI in identifying patterns from data I would otherwise overlook, and sorely want a local-only ecosystem where I can expose this data safely, privately, and securely to something like Qwen or Gemma for processing.

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly rejects using ChatGPT for sensitive health data, expressing strong distrust around privacy and control.
The commenter explicitly says Anthropic will never get their health data, a strong privacy/trust rejection.

evidence.excerpt: The product I want most is the ability to return to the late January 2026 version of Anthropic models.

annotations.rationale: Preferring an older version implies the current Anthropic models are seen as worse.

evidence.excerpt: I've been relying on Qwen and gemini flash lite, both more expensive than Deepseek.

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly says Gemini Flash Lite is more expensive than DeepSeek, a mild pricing criticism.

evidence.excerpt: Apparently according to Gemini, I, the only person in the world with my name (unique first name + long complex last name) am a professional soccer player. Yeah, be careful with answers you get from AIs.

annotations.rationale: The commenter reports an obviously wrong answer from Gemini and warns readers to be careful with AI answers.

evidence.excerpt: But Haiku just says "no one of that name seems to exist". So Haiku must be pretty pruned down.

annotations.rationale: The commenter criticizes Haiku for failing to identify someone that other models mostly got right, implying weaker capability.

evidence.excerpt: I've had so much fun writing small apps in pure JS and HTML witj Gemini (no harness or agent, just the free web chat) because it's forced me to keep my index.html below 1000 lines. I love the forced constraints. It's liberating.

annotations.rationale: The commenter is highly enthusiastic about using Gemini, calling the experience fun, lovable, and liberating.

evidence.excerpt: What a win for OpenAI - and what a loss for Google, just 2 years after paying $2.7bn to bring Noam back into the fold. Does not bode well for Gemini long-term...

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly calls the hire a win for OpenAI.
The commenter says the move does not bode well for Gemini long-term, expressing concern for Gemini's position.

evidence.excerpt: Really impressive by OAI if this reporting is accurate!

annotations.rationale: In the OpenAI story context, 'OAI' is a likely alias for OpenAI and the comment praises the move as impressive.

evidence.excerpt: We're very excited about this taking shape in Claude (in addition to the MCP spec, of course, where EMA is now a stable extension) and are looking to expand adoption to other identity providers and clients as well.

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly expresses excitement about the feature landing in Claude and broader adoption.

evidence.excerpt: Claude has been doing this magically for some of their built-in connectors (at least Slack I think) and the experience is pretty great.

annotations.rationale: The commenter praises Claude's connector behavior and says the experience is pretty great.

evidence.excerpt: people widely see Opus 4.5 in November last year - 8 months ago - as the first time agentic coding became viable broadly viable even with frontier hosted models.

annotations.rationale: The commenter credits Opus 4.5 with making agentic coding broadly viable, which is strong praise for model capability.
